opinion.h: UNITED STATES v. HALEY. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF TEXAS. No. 587. Decided February 24, 1959.
opinion.p: Solicitor General Rankin, Assistant Attorney General Hansen, Charles H. Weston, R. L. Farrington, Neil Brooks and Donald A. Campbell for the United States.
William F. Billings, James P. Donovan and Daniel L. O'Connor for appellee.
The judgment is reversed. Wickard v. Filburn, 317 U.S. 111.
358 U.S. 644 LANDMAN ET AL. v. MIEDZINSKI, SHERIFF, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF MARYLAND. No. 589. Decided February 24, 1959.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
F. Joseph Donohue and Harold C. Faulkner for appellants.
C. Ferdinand Sybert, Attorney General of Maryland, Stedman Prescott, Jr., Deputy Attorney General, and James H. Norris, Jr. for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
